perturbation.original_text: Purpose : means insert a very precise purpose eg the evaluation or establishment of a collaboration in respect of a particular project .
perturbation.changed_text: Purpose : means the evaluation or establishment of a collaboration in respect of a particular project or any other project that the Parties may agree upon verbally.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition requires a 'very precise purpose.' The added phrase 'or any other project that the Parties may agree upon verbally' contradicts this precision. It introduces ambiguity because verbal agreements are not clearly defined or documented, creating an in-text contradiction with the initial need for precision. The original text and the added text are contradictory, since precise and verbally agreed are contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Purpose' as having a 'very precise purpose'. The changed text adds 'or any other project that the Parties may agree upon verbally,' which introduces ambiguity and contradicts the requirement for precision. The 'Contradicted Text' outlines the recipient party's obligation to use the confidential information solely for the 'Purpose' as defined. Since the changed text broadens 'Purpose' to include verbally agreed-upon projects, this creates an in-text contradiction, because the confidentiality obligations now apply to a less well-defined scope, making the clause less enforceable and consistent. The definitions for 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' matches this scenario.

perturbation.original_text: Representative (s): means employees, agents, officers , professional advisers and Affiliates of the Recipient Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Representative (s): means employees, agents, officers , professional advisers and Affiliates of the Recipient Party. For the purposes of clause 3 only, 'Representatives' shall also include independent contractors.
perturbation.explanation: This modification adds independent contractors to the definition of 'Representatives' specifically for clause 3. This implies that, elsewhere in the agreement, independent contractors are *not* considered Representatives. This in-text contradiction impacts clause 3's interpretation and enforcement. This creates an in-text contradiction because elsewhere outside clause 3 they are not Representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'Representatives' adds 'independent contractors' specifically for clause 3. This implies that independent contractors are not considered 'Representatives' outside of clause 3. The contradicted text, specifically clause (d), states that the Recipient Party can disclose confidential information to its Representatives, and the Recipient Party is liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of the Agreement. Because independent contractors are only Representatives in clause 3, this creates a direct contradiction regarding whether the Recipient Party is liable for the actions of independent contractors outside of clause 3 when they receive confidential information. This meets the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the above, the Recipient Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Notwithstanding the above, the Recipient Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes' directly contradicts the initial requirement to return or destroy all Confidential Information. The contradiction creates an ambiguity in the requirement to return or erase, since there is an exception now. The added text is contradictory to the previous statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the above, the Recipient Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Recipient Party to return or destroy all Confidential Information. The changed text adds an exception allowing the Recipient Party to retain one copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the initial requirement to return or destroy *all* Confidential Information, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient Party shall have the rig ht to disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose, subject to the Recipient Party obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party to such disclosure and the Recipient Party agrees that it shall procure that th e third party shall first enter into a written acknowledgement and undertaking of confidentiality in favour of both Parties to this Agreement and in a form which is acceptable to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient Party shall have the rig ht to disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose without the need to obtain consent from the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for prior written consent from the Disclosing Party directly contradicts the original text's insistence on obtaining such consent. This change creates a clear in-text contradiction. The original text states there must be written consent, and the new text does not need to obtain consent, which are contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure to Third P arties The Recipient Party shall have the rig ht to disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose without the need to obtain consent from the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text removes the requirement of obtaining prior written consent, directly contradicting the Contradicted Text which states 'The Recipient Party shall have the rig ht to disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose without the need to obtain consent from the disclosing party.' The original text states there must be written consent from the disclosing party, while the change and contradicted text states there is no need to obtain consent from the disclosing party. This is a direct contradiction, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability of the contract as outlined in the perturbation explanation. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is present.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of five (5) years from the termination of this A greement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of one (1) year from the termination of this A greement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'five (5) years' to 'one (1) year' for the duration of confidentiality obligations after termination creates an in-text contradiction. This significantly reduces the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party. The original text states the confidentiality last 5 years, and the new text reduces that duration to 1 year which is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of one (1) year from the termination of this A greement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that obligations continue for five years after termination. The changed text modifies this to one year. The contradicted text also states one year. The change from five years to one year directly contradicts the original obligation period, creating a clear inconsistency, even though the contradicted text is now in agreement with the changed text. The change causes the contradiction to the original contract.
